journal_entry.description: Acquisition of land with building for factory construction, financed by instalment note payable
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, land acquired for construction of a new factory is capitalized as Land (non-depreciable asset). The existing building to be demolished is not a separate asset; its cost is included in the land cost as part of site preparation. The purchase price of $60,000,000 is paid via 6 annual instalments of $10,000,000. Under IFRS, the liability must be recorded at present value using the market interest rate of 6%. PV = $10,000,000 × [(1 - (1.06)^-6) / 0.06] = $10,000,000 × 4.9173 = $49,173,000. The difference between face value ($60,000,000) and present value ($49,173,000) represents implicit interest that will be recognized over the payment period. The land is recorded at its cash-equivalent price (present value of the liability). Debit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) = 1 asset increase; Credit tuple (1,0,0,1,0,0) = 1 liability increase + 1 asset decrease is overridden because there is no asset decrease at acquisition—only an asset increase (land) and liability increase (note payable). This is a non-cash transaction with no immediate cash outflow.

output.reason:
Under IFRS, the land acquisition is recorded at present value. The debit side records:
1. Land (asset increase) - The land is acquired for the purpose of constructing a new factory. The existing building to be demolished is part of the land cost, not a separate asset, since it has no future economic benefit and will be removed immediately. This is recorded at the present value of the payment obligation ($10M × 6 years at 6%).
The difference between face value ($60M) and present value creates a discount that will be unwound over time through interest expense, but that discount is a contra-liability account on the CREDIT side of this initial acquisition entry, not the debit side.
Therefore, only ONE debit line: Land = asset increase.

debit_classifier.reason:
Under IFRS, the land acquisition is recorded at present value. The debit side records:
1. Land (asset increase) - The land is acquired for the purpose of constructing a new factory. The existing building to be demolished is part of the land cost, not a separate asset, since it has no future economic benefit and will be removed immediately. This is recorded at the present value of the payment obligation ($10M × 6 years at 6%).
The difference between face value ($60M) and present value creates a discount that will be unwound over time through interest expense, but that discount is a contra-liability account on the CREDIT side of this initial acquisition entry, not the debit side.
Therefore, only ONE debit line: Land = asset increase.

output.reason:
This transaction involves acquiring land for $60,000,000 to be paid in instalments. On the credit side:

1. **Asset decrease (Cash)**: $10,000,000 - The first instalment payment made at the end of the year of acquisition represents cash leaving the business.

2. **Liability increase (Long-term payable)**: The remaining 5 instalments totaling $50,000,000 create a financial liability. Under IFRS, when payment terms differ from immediate settlement, the liability must be recorded at present value (discounted at 6% market rate). This represents a liability increase.

The existing building to be demolished is not separately recognized - its value is included in the land cost as site preparation under IAS 16. The credit side shows: one asset decrease (cash payment) and one liability increase (present value of future instalments).

The existing building to be demolished is not separately recognized - its value is included in the land cost as site preparation under IAS 16. The credit side shows: one asset decrease (cash payment) and one liability increase (present value of future instalments).

output.reason: Entry correctly applies IFRS 9 and IAS 16. Land recorded at present value of payment obligation: $10M × 4.9173 annuity factor = $49,173,000. Note Payable correctly recorded at same present value using 6% market rate. The $10,827,000 difference between face value ($60M) and PV will be recognized as interest expense over payment period. Building demolition cost properly included in land cost per IAS 16. No cash payment at acquisition (first instalment due at year-end). Entry balances. Not a taxable transaction. All accounts and amounts correct.
